game_info.title: Fruit Match - Physics Puzzle
game_info.concept: Match Game is a physics-based puzzle where players strategically drop various fruits into a container, aiming to merge identical fruits upon collision to create progressively larger ones. The central mechanic involves careful placement using left/right swipes and drops, as two matching fruits combine into the next fruit in a predetermined sequence, culminating in a giant watermelon. The game's primary rule dictates that if fruits spill over the container's top edge, the game ends, emphasizing spatial management and foresight. Progression is measured by achieving high scores through successful merges and the ultimate challenge of creating the largest watermelon, rather than distinct levels. Controls are simple swipe-and-drop gestures. Monetization, while not explicitly detailed, typically involves rewarded video ads for retries or power-ups, and optional in-app purchases for ad removal or cosmetic enhancements.
game_info.description: Match Game is a physics-based fruit merging puzzle. Drop fruits into a container where identical fruits merge into larger ones when they collide. Progress through the fruit sequence: cherry → strawberry → grape → orange → apple → pear → peach → pineapple → melon → watermelon. Score points with each merge and aim to create the ultimate watermelon. If fruits pile up and cross the danger line at the top, the game ends. Plan your drops carefully to manage space and maximize merges!
game_info.controls:
LEFT ARROW or A: Move the preview fruit left
RIGHT ARROW or D: Move the preview fruit right
SPACE or DOWN ARROW or S: Drop the current fruit
ESC: Pause/Unpause the game
ENTER: Start the game (from start screen)
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic fruit dropping and merging mechanics. This ensures fruits drop correctly, physics behaves realistically, and two identical fruits merge into the next tier fruit. This is the core mechanic validation test.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Drop fruits in a controlled pattern, placing identical fruits adjacent to each other. Wait for physics to settle between drops. Drop pairs of cherries to create strawberries, then strawberries to create grapes, testing the merge chain. Verify score increases with each merge.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Fruits should drop smoothly, settle with realistic physics, and identical fruits should merge on contact creating the next fruit in sequence. Score should increase with each successful merge. No errors or visual glitches should occur. Test is successful when multiple merge tiers are achieved without issues.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by creating a watermelon. This validates the complete fruit progression chain and ensures the game recognizes and celebrates reaching the highest tier fruit. Tests both gameplay depth and win state handling.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Systematically work through the fruit progression by strategically dropping and merging fruits. Start with cherries, merge up through all tiers methodically. Place fruits carefully to enable cascading merges. Focus on creating the final watermelon through optimal placement strategy.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully creating a watermelon should trigger a win state. The game should display "GAME_OVER_WIN" phase, show a victory message with final score, and allow restart. The watermelon should be visibly the largest fruit. Test succeeds when watermelon creation is achieved and properly recognized.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing game over condition when fruits exceed the danger line. This validates the lose condition triggers correctly when the container becomes too full, ensuring the spatial management challenge works as intended.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately drop fruits in a way that stacks them vertically without creating many merges. Fill the container quickly by placing fruits on top of each other. Avoid strategic placement that would enable merges and space clearing.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When fruits stack high enough to cross the danger line at the top of the container, the game should enter "GAME_OVER_LOSE" state. A game over message should display with the final score and restart option. Test succeeds when the lose condition triggers appropriately.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing fruit preview and positioning controls. Validates that the preview fruit moves smoothly left and right before dropping, stays within bounds, and accurately shows where the fruit will land.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Move the preview fruit back and forth across the full width of the container before dropping. Test boundary limits by holding left/right at edges. Drop fruits at various positions to ensure preview position matches drop location.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Preview fruit should move smoothly with arrow keys, stop at container boundaries, and the dropped fruit should appear exactly where the preview indicated. No fruits should spawn outside the container. Test succeeds with accurate preview-to-drop correspondence.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state transitions. Ensures pausing freezes physics simulation, displays pause overlay, and resumes correctly. Also validates all phase transitions work properly.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Start game, drop several fruits, then pause with ESC. Verify physics stops. Unpause and confirm game continues normally. Test pause during various game states including mid-drop. Repeat pause/unpause cycles multiple times.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Physics should completely stop during pause with no fruit movement. A pause overlay should display. Unpausing should resume exactly where left off. All game elements should remain consistent through pause cycles. Test succeeds with seamless pause/resume functionality.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing score system and progression feedback. Validates that points are correctly awarded for merges, with higher tier merges giving more points. Ensures score display updates accurately and provides meaningful progression feedback.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Create merges of different fruit tiers and track score increases. Compare points awarded for low-tier merges (cherries) versus high-tier merges (melons). Create multiple merges in succession to test score accumulation and display updates.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Score should increase with each merge, with higher-tier merges awarding more points. Score display should update immediately after each merge. The scoring should feel rewarding and provide clear progression feedback. Test succeeds when scoring accurately reflects merge achievements.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing edge cases and physics stability. Validates game handles unusual situations like rapid dropping, multiple simultaneous merges, and chain reactions without errors or performance degradation.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Game should handle rapid inputs without lag or errors. Multiple simultaneous merges should process correctly. Physics should remain stable with many objects. Frame rate should stay smooth. Test succeeds when edge cases are handled gracefully without crashes or visual glitches.
